2026-04-22T08:47:42.5806020Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T08:47:42.5806607Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T08:47:42.5807046Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T08:47:42.5807418Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T08:47:42.5807731Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T08:47:42.5807983Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T08:47:42.5808145Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T08:47:42.5808327Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T08:47:42.5808500Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T08:47:42.5808730Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-22T08:47:42.5809214Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T08:47:42.5809472Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

The package now ships new preview capabilities under the updated API surface.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Examples** section in `README.md` contains only a generic link to `.NET Management Library Code Samples` with no service-specific inline code snippets showing how to create, get, list, or delete a `ComputeFleet` resource\\n- The **Key concepts** section is a single generic link with no explanation of the primary resource types (`ComputeFleetResource`, `ComputeFleetCollection`) or how they fit into the Azure resource hierarchy\\n- There is a non-standard **Documentation** section in the README that is not part of the required template; its useful links (Quickstart, API References, Authentication) should be moved into the appropriate sections (Getting started / Next steps) and the section removed\\n- No `tests/Samples/` directory exists with curated samples backed by `#region Snippet:` tags; only auto-generated samples live in `tests/Generated/Samples/`\\n- The README does not reference the new `2025-07-01-preview` API version or highlight what new capabilities it brings\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/README.md` (edit)\\n\\nReplace the existing **Key concepts** and **Examples** sections and remove the non-standard **Documentation** section. Replace with the following:\\n\\n````markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet` uses the following primary types:\\n\\n- **`ComputeFleetResource`** – represents a single Compute Fleet resource. Use it to get, update, delete, or list virtual machine instances in a fleet.\\n- **`ComputeFleetCollection`** – a collection scoped to a resource group that lets you create and list Compute Fleet resources.\\n\\nFor a general introduction to Azure Resource Manager client libraries, see the [Azure Resource Manager SDK overview]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show basic usage patterns. For the full list of operations, see the [API reference]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n\\n### Create a Compute Fleet\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ComputeFleet_CreateFleet\\n```\\n\\n### Get a Compute Fleet\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ComputeFleet_GetFleet\\n```\\n\\n### List Compute Fleets in a resource group\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ComputeFleet_ListFleets\\n```\\n\\n### Delete a Compute Fleet\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ComputeFleet_DeleteFleet\\n```\\n````\\n\\nAlso remove the non-standard **Documentation** section (the block between `## Key concepts` and `## Examples`) and integrate the quickstart link into the **Getting started** → **Authenticate the Client** sub-section.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate the file `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_BasicOperations.cs` with:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_BasicOperations : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task CreateFleet()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeFleet_CreateFleet\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription\\n                .GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ComputeFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetComputeFleets();\\n\\n            ComputeFleetData fleetData = new ComputeFleetData(new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                Properties = new ComputeFleetProperties(\\n                    new ComputeFleetVmProfile(),\\n                    new[]\\n                    {\\n                        new ComputeFleetVmSizeProfile(\\u0026#34;Standard_D2s_v3\\u0026#34;)\\n                    })\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myComputeFleet\\u0026#34;, fleetData);\\n            ComputeFleetResource fleet = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created fleet: {fleet.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task GetFleet()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeFleet_GetFleet\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription\\n                .GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ComputeFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetComputeFleets();\\n            ComputeFleetResource fleet = await fleets.GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myComputeFleet\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Fleet: {fleet.Data.Name}, Location: {fleet.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task ListFleets()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeFleet_ListFleets\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription\\n                .GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (ComputeFleetResource fleet in resourceGroup.GetComputeFleets())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine(fleet.Data.Name);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task DeleteFleet()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeFleet_DeleteFleet\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription\\n                .GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ComputeFleetResource fleet = await resourceGroup\\n                .GetComputeFleets()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myComputeFleet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await fleet.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            Console.WriteLine(\\u0026#34;Fleet deleted.\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe snippet names must match the README placeholders exactly: `ComputeFleet_CreateFleet`, `ComputeFleet_GetFleet`, The package now ships new preview capabilities under the updated API surface.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Examples** section in `README.md` contains only a generic link to `.NET Management Library Code Samples` with no service-specific inline code snippets showing how to create, get, list, or delete a `ComputeFleet` resource\\n- The **Key concepts** section is a single generic link with no explanation of the primary resource types (`ComputeFleetResource`, `ComputeFleetCollection`) or how they fit into the Azure resource hierarchy\\n- There is a non-standard **Documentation** section in the README that is not part of the required template; its useful links (Quickstart, API References, Authentication) should be moved into the appropriate sections (Getting started / Next steps) and the section removed\\n- No `tests/Samples/` directory exists with curated samples backed by `#region Snippet:` tags; only auto-generated samples live in `tests/Generated/Samples/`\\n- The README does not reference the new `2025-07-01-preview` API version or highlight what new capabilities it brings\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/computefleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeFleet/README.md` (edit)\\n\\nReplace the existing **Key concepts** and **Examples** sections and remove the non-standard **Documentation** section.
